pi_items.source: Verified across setup_task.sh, export_result.sh, verifier.py, and task.json description - all files consistently reference this path

Cherry MX specification: Switch plate hole is 14 ± 0.05 mm square (verified via https://sparkfun.com/datasheets and https://wiki.ai03.com). Task description explicitly states 14.00 mm x 14.00 mm switch cutouts.

Cherry MX standard pitch: Official specification is 19.05mm center-to-center (verified via multiple sources including https://wiki.ai03.com and https://null-src.com/posts/keyboard-design-cheatsheet/). This equals 3/4 inch (25.4mm/4 * 3 = 19.05mm), also known as '1 keyboard unit (u)'.
Task description explicitly states '3 columns x 2 rows' and 'Arrangement: **3 columns x 2 rows**.' Verified in task.json description line.
Task description explicitly states '3 columns x 2 rows'. Verified in task.json description line.

Task description explicitly states 'The **center of the top-left switch** must be exactly at **(40, 80)**.' Geometry verified: plate bounds calculate to X: 27.0 to 91.1, Y: 47.95 to 93.0 with 6mm margin.
Task description explicitly states 'The plate edge must be exactly **6.00 mm** away from the outer edges of the switch cutouts on all sides.' Verified by geometry calculation.
Task description explicitly states 'Apply a **3.00 mm radius fillet** to all four corners of the plate.' Verified in verifier.py which checks for LWPOLYLINE bulge (arc representation).

Task description explicitly states '**3.20 mm diameter**' for mounting holes. Verified as standard: M3 clearance hole (close fit) is 3.2mm per ISO metric specifications (https://monsterbolts.com and https://amesweb.info). Common in mechanical keyboard design via https://wiki.ai03.com.

Task description explicitly states 'Center each hole **3.50 mm** inward from the straight edges of the plate corners.' Verified in verifier.py lines 235-240 which calculates hole positions with this offset.

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic task creating a mechanical keyboard macropad mounting plate in DXF format. All key specifications use industry-standard Cherry MX dimensions: 14.0 mm square switch cutouts (Cherry MX standard ±0.05mm), 19.05 mm pitch spacing (official Cherry specification, also 1 keyboard unit), 3.2 mm diameter mounting holes (M3 standard clearance hole close fit per ISO metric specs), and a 3x2 grid layout. Additional design parameters include 6.0 mm margin from switch edges to plate outline, 3.0 mm corner radius fillets, and 3.5 mm inward offset for corner-mounted holes. The output file path is /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/macropad_plate.dxf.
